book_summary: Exodus is complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The book runs from names and oppression through liberation, wilderness formation, Sinai covenant, tabernacle/priesthood instructions, golden calf rupture, Moses' intercession, renewed covenant, actual tabernacle construction, and glory filling the dwelling. Key themes include YHWH's seeing/hearing/knowing, liberation from forced service, Pesach/Passover, mixed multitude, one Torah for native and sojourner, manna/Sabbath enoughness, Jethro's distributed leadership, covenant identity as priestly kingdom/holy nation, liberation-shaped Torah/instruction, justice for vulnerable people, sacred architecture, priestly representation, Spirit-filled craft, Sabbath as covenant sign, covenant rupture, intercession, mercy and accountability, and Divine presence guiding all journeys.

translation_notes: Hebrew-first approach required: Shemot/Names, YHWH, Elohim, avad/avodah, berit, ruach, pesach, mishkan, kavod, chesed where relevant.
YHWH name disclosure in Exodus 3:14 preserves Hebrew verbal ambiguity: I AM / I WILL BE.
Pharaoh hardening language must track Hebrew verbs carefully: hazaq, kaved, qashah require later lexical audit.
Do not demonize Egyptians ethnically; critique Pharaoh, empire, forced labor, and fear-based domination.
Ten Words should be framed as liberation-shaped covenant instruction, not abstract legalism.
Ancient slavery, patriarchy, violence, and property laws in Exodus 21-23 require source preservation plus anti-domination safeguards. They must not be treated as timeless moral ideals.
Land promise and displacement language require later ethical audit and must not be used to justify modern dispossession or violence.
Tabernacle begins with willing-hearted contribution, not coercive extraction.
Mishkan/sanctuary is for YHWH dwelling in the people's midst after liberation.
Priestly garments symbolize representative burden-bearing, not status theater: names of Israel on shoulders and heart.
Craft is Spirit-filled wisdom: Bezalel, Oholiab, wise-hearted artisans, and wise-hearted women are theological actors.
Sabbath brackets tabernacle work: even sacred construction stops for covenant rest.
The more-than-enough offering in Exodus 36 is restrained by Moses; sacred work must not become endless extraction.
Women’s mirrors become the bronze basin in Exodus 38, preserving women’s contribution and service at the tent entrance.
Sacred materials are inventoried, emphasizing accountability and transparency.
Exodus ends with glory filling the dwelling and cloud/fire guiding all journeys: presence is the achieved goal of liberation.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Exodus 3:14 YHWH/Ehyeh asher ehyeh requires deep lexical and reception-history audit.
Exodus 4:21 and hardening motif should receive verb-by-verb audit across Exodus.
Exodus land promise / named peoples requires ethical and historical reception audit before later conquest texts.
Exodus 12 Pesach/Passover terminology and calendar reset require Hebrew ritual/source audit.
Exodus 20 Ten Words require Decalogue tradition comparison with Deuteronomy 5.
Exodus 21 slavery/servant laws require ancient Near Eastern context and anti-slavery reception-history audit.
Exodus 22:18 sorcery prohibition requires dangerous reception-history safeguards.
Exodus 23 land/displacement language requires ethical audit before any application.
Exodus 24 covenant ratification/blood language requires ritual-source audit.
Exodus 25-40 tabernacle design/construction requires ancient cultic/material/source audit.
Exodus 25 kapporet/cover/atonement-cover terminology requires lexical and later reception audit.
Exodus 28 Urim/Thummim requires ancient divination/priestly discernment audit.
Exodus 29 ordination/filling-hand ritual requires priestly source audit.
Exodus 31 and 35 Sabbath penalty language requires ethical/reception-history audit.
Exodus 32 golden calf and Levite violence require strong anti-violence reception-history audit.
Exodus 33 face/presence tension requires lexical and theological audit.
Exodus 34:6-7 YHWH character formula requires deep lexical and cross-canonical audit.
Exodus 34 shining face/veil requires reception-history audit, especially later Pauline usage.
Exodus 36 more-than-enough offering requires anti-extraction stewardship note.
Exodus 38 women’s mirrors/basin requires gender/service/source audit.
Exodus 40 glory filling dwelling requires canonical presence-theme crosslink audit.
next_actions: Exodus complete. Next recommended common-scaffold book: Leviticus, or create metadata/torah-progress.json after more Torah books are completed.
